data.current_description: Returns permissions that a caller has on the specified resource. If the resource does not exist, this will return an empty set of permissions, not a `NOT_FOUND` error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.
data.summary_suggestion: Test IAM permissions on API Gateway resource
data.description_suggestion: Tests which IAM permissions the authenticated caller has on the specified API Gateway resource. Returns a list of permissions the caller possesses. If the resource does not exist, returns an empty permission set rather than a NOT_FOUND error. This operation is intended for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization enforcement. Note: This operation may fail open without warning and should not be used as the sole authorization mechanism.
data.reasoning: Original had no summary, making it difficult for AI agents to quickly understand the operation's purpose. Description was incomplete regarding output details and lacked clarity about the operation's limitations and intended use cases. Enhanced summary provides immediate context about the operation type and resource. Improved description clarifies the return value structure, edge cases, and explicitly warns about authorization limitations to prevent misuse by AI agents.

data.current_description: Deletes a long-running operation. This method indicates that the client is no longer interested in the operation result. It does not cancel the operation. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`.

This method does not cancel the operation; it only removes the operation from the server's operation list. Requires the operation name as input. Returns an empty response on success. Note: If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`.

message: Operation 'apigateway.projects.locations.operations.cancel' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary was empty. Description was comprehensive but lacked clarity on the return value (empty response) and didn't explicitly mention the request schema (ApigatewayCancelOperationRequest). Added summary and clarified the response structure and request schema for better AI agent understanding of what to expect.

data.current_description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of `1`, corresponding to `Code.CANCELLED`.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates asynchronous cancellation of a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel but success is not guaranteed. Requires the operation name and optional ApigatewayCancelOperationRequest body. Returns an empty response on success. The operation is not deleted but transitions to a CANCELLED state with an error status. Use GetOperation to verify cancellation status. Note: If the server doesn't support this method, it returns `google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED`.
data.reasoning: Original summary was empty. Description was comprehensive but lacked clarity on the return value (empty response) and didn't explicitly mention the request schema (ApigatewayCancelOperationRequest). Added summary and clarified the response structure and request schema for better AI agent understanding of what to expect.

data.current_description: Sets the access control policy on the specified resource. Replaces any existing policy. Can return `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, and `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors.
data.description_suggestion: Sets or replaces the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for a specified API Gateway resource. Requires the resource name and an ApigatewaySetIamPolicyRequest body containing the new policy. Replaces any existing policy completely. Returns the updated ApigatewayPolicy object. May return `NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`, or `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors.
